mission.title: Install and Configure MetalLB (Bare-metal Load Balancer)
mission.description: MetalLB is a load-balancer implementation for bare metal Kubernetes clusters, utilizing standard routing protocols. It is ideal for on-premises Kubernetes setups where cloud load balancers are not available.

steps.title: Install MetalLB using Helm
steps.description:
First, add the MetalLB Helm repository and install MetalLB. Ensure you are using Helm version 3.5.0 or later. Run the following commands:
```bash
helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb
helm repo update
helm install metallb metallb/metallb --version v0.15.3
```

steps.title: Configure MetalLB with Layer 2 mode
steps.description:
Create a ConfigMap to configure MetalLB. Replace the IP range with your own available IPs:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    layer2:
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify MetalLB installation
steps.description:
Check that MetalLB pods are running and ready:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system
kubectl get svc -n metallb-system
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration for LoadBalancer service
steps.description:
Create a sample LoadBalancer service to test MetalLB:
```bash
kubectl expose deployment your-deployment --type=LoadBalancer --name=your-service
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall MetalLB
uninstall.description:
To uninstall MetalLB, run the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall metallb
``` Then, delete the ConfigMap if it was created:
```bash
kubectl delete configmap config -n metallb-system
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade MetalLB to v0.15
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml
```
To upgrade to v0.15.3, run:
```bash
helm upgrade metallb metallb/metallb --version v0.15.3
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system
```

troubleshooting.title: MetalLB pods are not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the MetalLB pods for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs -n metallb-system -l app=metallb
```
Common issues include misconfiguration in the ConfigMap or insufficient permissions.

troubleshooting.title: LoadBalancer service not getting an external IP
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure that the IP range defined in the ConfigMap is within the subnet of your network. Check the service status:
```bash
kubectl get svc your-service
```

troubleshooting.title: MetalLB not responding to ARP requests
troubleshooting.description:
Verify that your network is configured to allow ARP broadcasts. Check the MetalLB logs for ARP-related messages:
```bash
kubectl logs -n metallb-system -l app=metallb | grep ARP
```

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for additional IP address management features and improved stability.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old configuration format in favor of a new structured format.

versionNotes.changes: Initial support for Layer 2 mode and basic service functionalities.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of MetalLB will have the MetalLB pods running in the 'metallb-system' namespace and LoadBalancer services successfully receiving external IPs from the configured range.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb
helm repo update
helm install metallb metallb/metallb --version v0.15.3

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    layer2:
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system
kubectl get svc -n metallb-system

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and access to it via kubectl.
